Details: Inactivation of Cryptosporidium in water by chlorine dioxide can be very effective (1- to over 3-log inactivation) where the effectiveness is dependent on temperature, chlorine dioxide dose, contact time, and water quality. Chlorine dioxide is in the LT2 microbial tool box for control of Cryptosporidium. Temperature has the most significant impact on efficacy of chlorine dioxide for Cryptosporidium inactivation. In addition, chlorine dioxide is less effective is highly turbid natural waters with high (>4 mg/L) TOC (Total Organic Carbon) levels. Chlorine dioxide has been shown to display synergism if applied with ozone, chlorine, or monochloramine in disinfection schemes.
